1→//! Session types shared between daemon and server.
     2→
     3→use chrono::{DateTime, Utc};
     4→use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
     5→use uuid::Uuid;
     6→
     7→/// Type of agent session.
     8→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
     9→#[serde(rename_all = "kebab-case")]
    10→pub enum SessionType {
    11→    Arcan,
    12→    ClaudeCode,
    13→    Codex,
    14→}
    15→
    16→impl std::fmt::Display for SessionType {
    17→    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {
    18→        match self {
    19→            Self::Arcan => write!(f, "arcan"),
    20→            Self::ClaudeCode => write!(f, "claude-code"),
    21→            Self::Codex => write!(f, "codex"),
    22→        }
    23→    }
    24→}
    25→
    26→/// Session lifecycle status.
    27→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
    28→#[serde(rename_all = "lowercase")]
    29→pub enum SessionStatus {
    30→    Active,
    31→    Idle,
    32→    Completed,
    33→    Failed,
    34→}
    35→
    36→/// Information about a session on the relay node.
    37→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
    38→#[serde(rename_all = "camelCase")]
    39→pub struct SessionInfo {
    40→    pub id: Uuid,
    41→    pub session_type: SessionType,
    42→    pub status: SessionStatus,
    43→    pub name: String,
    44→    pub workdir: String,
    45→    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    46→    pub model: Option<String>,
    47→    pub created_at: DateTime<Utc>,
    48→}
    49→
    50→/// Configuration for spawning a new session.
    51→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
    52→#[serde(rename_all = "camelCase")]
    53→pub struct SpawnConfig {
    54→    pub name: String,
    55→    #[serde(default = "default_workdir")]
    56→    pub workdir: String,
    57→    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    58→    pub model: Option<String>,
    59→    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    60→    pub session_id: Option<Uuid>,
    61→}
    62→
    63→fn default_workdir() -> String {
    64→    std::env::var("HOME").unwrap_or_else(|_| ".".to_string())
    65→}
    66→

[workspace]
resolver = "2"
members = [
    "crates/life-relay-core",
    "crates/life-relay-api",
    "crates/life-relayd",
]

[workspace.package]
version = "0.1.0"
edition = "2024"
rust-version = "1.85"
license = "MIT"
authors = ["Broomva Tech <contact@broomva.tech>"]
repository = "https://github.com/broomva/life-relay"
homepage = "https://broomva.tech"
keywords = ["agent", "relay", "remote-sessions", "ai"]
categories = ["command-line-utilities", "development-tools"]

[workspace.lints.rust]
unsafe_code = "deny"

[workspace.lints.clippy]
dbg_macro = "warn"
print_stdout = "warn"
print_stderr = "warn"
todo = "warn"
unimplemented = "warn"
needless_pass_by_value = "warn"
cloned_instead_of_copied = "warn"
redundant_clone = "warn"
redundant_closure_for_method_calls = "warn"
unnested_or_patterns = "warn"
trivially_copy_pass_by_ref = "warn"
implicit_clone = "warn"
manual_let_else = "warn"
large_enum_variant = "warn"
doc_markdown = "warn"
semicolon_if_nothing_returned = "warn"
match_wildcard_for_single_variants = "warn"

[workspace.dependencies]
# Core
serde = { version = "1", features = ["derive"] }
serde_json = "1"
thiserror = "2"
chrono = { version = "0.4", features = ["serde"] }
uuid = { version = "1", features = ["v4", "serde"] }

# Async
tokio = { version = "1", features = ["macros", "rt-multi-thread", "signal", "sync", "process", "io-util", "time"] }
tokio-stream = { version = "0.1", features = ["sync"] }
tokio-tungstenite = { version = "0.26", features = ["native-tls"] }
futures-util = "0.3"

# HTTP
axum = { version = "0.8", features = ["macros", "ws"] }
tower-http = { version = "0.6", features = ["cors", "trace"] }
reqwest = { version = "0.12", features = ["json"] }
reqwest-eventsource = "0.6"

# PTY
portable-pty = "0.8"

# CLI
clap = { version = "4", features = ["derive"] }
toml = "0.9"
anyhow = "1"
dirs = "6"
hostname = "0.4"
async-trait = "0.1"

# Observability
tracing = "0.1"
tracing-subscriber = { version = "0.3", features = ["env-filter"] }

# Internal crates
life-relay-core = { version = "0.1.0", path = "crates/life-relay-core" }
life-relay-api = { version = "0.1.0", path = "crates/life-relay-api" }

[profile.dev]
opt-level = 0
debug = true
split-debuginfo = "unpacked"

[profile.release]
opt-level = 3
lto = true
codegen-units = 1
strip = true
panic = "abort"

# Life Relay — Web-Based Remote Agent Sessions

> Open-source remote access to agent sessions (Claude Code, Codex, Arcan) via broomva.tech PWA.
> Inspired by [Omnara](https://omnara.com) but multi-provider, self-hostable, and integrated into the Life Agent OS.

## Problem

Agent CLI sessions (Claude Code, Codex, Arcan) run locally in a terminal. Monitoring, approving permissions, or sending input requires physical access to the machine. Omnara solves this with a $20/mo proprietary web/mobile app. We want the same capability as part of the open-source Life ecosystem.

## Architecture

```
Browser (broomva.tech PWA)
    |
    +-- SSE <-- /api/relay/sessions/{id}/stream   (output)
    +-- POST -> /api/relay/sessions/{id}/input    (commands)
         |
    +----+----------------------------+
    |   Redis Pub/Sub + Postgres      |  (Vercel infra)
    +----+----------------------------+
         | WebSocket (outbound, TLS+JWT)
    +----+----------------------------+
    |   relayd  (Rust daemon)         |  (user's machine)
    |                                 |
    |   Adapters:                     |
    |   +-- ArcanAdapter  -> arcand HTTP API (SSE events)
    |   +-- ClaudeAdapter -> tmux/PTY (output capture + keystrokes)
    |   +-- CodexAdapter  -> tmux/PTY
    +----------------------------------+
```

### Key Design Decisions

1. **Outbound WebSocket relay** — relayd connects *out* to broomva.tech. Works behind NAT/firewalls with zero user configuration.
2. **Redis pub/sub bridges WS<->SSE** — matches broomva.tech's existing `resumable-stream` pattern from chat.
3. **Separate from arcand** — relay is a connectivity layer, not an agent runtime. It *calls* arcand's HTTP API for Arcan sessions.
4. **Device Authorization for registration** — reuses existing `/device` page and `deviceAuthCode` table.
5. **Wire protocol** — JSON over WebSocket, bidirectional `ServerMessage`/`DaemonMessage` enums.

## Crate: `life-relay`

Location: `core/life/relay/`

```
relay/
  Cargo.toml          # tokio, tokio-tungstenite, portable-pty, serde, reqwest
  src/
    main.rs           # CLI: relayd auth | start | stop | status
    config.rs         # ~/.config/life/relay/ config + credentials
    auth.rs           # Device auth flow (opens browser, polls for token)
    connection.rs     # WS client with auto-reconnect + exponential backoff
    protocol.rs       # Wire protocol types (serde JSON)
    registry.rs       # Local session registry (JSON file)
    daemon.rs         # Main event loop: WS recv -> dispatch to adapters
    adapters/
      mod.rs          # SessionAdapter trait
      arcan.rs        # HTTP client to local arcand
      claude.rs       # Claude Code via tmux/PTY
      codex.rs        # Codex CLI via tmux/PTY
      pty.rs          # Shared portable-pty helpers
```

### Dependencies

- `tokio` + `tokio-tungstenite` — async runtime + WebSocket
- `portable-pty` — cross-platform PTY spawning (same as Mission Control)
- `reqwest` + `reqwest-eventsource` — HTTP + SSE client for ArcanAdapter
- `serde` + `serde_json` — wire protocol serialization
- `clap` — CLI argument parsing

## Wire Protocol

```rust
// Server -> Daemon (commands from web UI)
enum ServerMessage {
    Spawn { session_type: String, config: SpawnConfig },
    Input { session_id: String, data: String },
    Resize { session_id: String, cols: u16, rows: u16 },
    Approve { session_id: String, approval_id: String, approved: bool },
    Kill { session_id: String },
    ListSessions,
    Ping,
}

// Daemon -> Server (events from local sessions)
enum DaemonMessage {
    Output { session_id: String, data: String, seq: u64 },
    SessionCreated { session: SessionInfo },
    SessionEnded { session_id: String, reason: String },
    ApprovalRequest { session_id: String, approval_id: String, capability: String },
    SessionList { sessions: Vec<SessionInfo> },
    NodeInfo { name: String, hostname: String, capabilities: Vec<String> },
    Pong,
    Error { code: String, message: String },
}
```

## Auth Flow

1. User logs into broomva.tech (Better Auth)
2. Installs relayd: `cargo install life-relay`
3. `relayd auth` -> opens browser to `/device?code=XXXX`
4. User approves -> JWT stored in `~/.config/life/relay/credentials.json`
5. `relayd start` -> outbound WS to `wss://broomva.tech/api/relay/connect`
6. Console shows node online at `/console/relay`

## Session Types

| Type | Adapter | How it works |
|------|---------|--------------|
| `arcan` | ArcanAdapter | HTTP proxy to local `arcand` — SSE events forwarded as WS frames |
| `claude-code` | ClaudeAdapter | PTY spawn of `claude` CLI — continuous output capture + keystroke injection |
| `codex` | CodexAdapter | PTY spawn of `codex` CLI — same pattern as Claude |

## broomva.tech Integration

### API Routes

| Route | Method | Purpose |
|-------|--------|---------|
| `/api/relay/connect` | WS | relayd connects here |
| `/api/relay/nodes` | GET | List user's relay nodes |
| `/api/relay/sessions` | GET | List sessions across nodes |
| `/api/relay/sessions/[id]/stream` | GET | SSE stream of session output |
| `/api/relay/sessions/[id]/input` | POST | Send input to session |
| `/api/relay/sessions/[id]/spawn` | POST | Spawn new session |

### Console Pages

- `/console/relay` — node list + session overview
- `/console/relay/[nodeId]` — node detail with sessions
- `/console/relay/session/[id]` — terminal view + input bar

### Database Tables

- `RelayNode` — registered machines (userId, name, hostname, status, lastSeenAt)
- `RelaySession` — sessions (nodeId, sessionType, status, workdir, remoteSessionId, lastSequence, model)

## Comparison with Omnara

| | Omnara | Life Relay |
|---|--------|------------|
| Price | $20/mo | Free (open source) |
| Providers | Claude Code, Codex | Claude Code, Codex, Arcan |
| Access | Proprietary app | broomva.tech PWA |
| Voice | Built-in | Not in MVP |
| Self-host | No | Yes (Docker compose) |
| Auth profiles | Single account | Per-session profiles |

## Comparison with claude-remote-sessions

The `claude-remote-sessions` skill (Discord/Telegram) is the **precursor**. Relay evolves the concept:

| | claude-remote-sessions | Life Relay |
|---|----------------------|------------|
| UI | Discord/Telegram | Web browser (PWA) |
| Transport | Discord plugin MCP | WebSocket relay |
| Session management | tmux + bash scripts | Rust daemon (portable-pty) |
| Multi-provider | Claude Code only | Claude Code + Codex + Arcan |
| Auth | Per-channel OAuth profiles | Device auth + JWT |
| Streaming | tmux pane capture (polling) | Continuous PTY read (real-time) |

## Implementation Phases

- **Phase 1 (MVP)**: relayd scaffold + ClaudeAdapter + WS relay + basic console UI
- **Phase 2**: ArcanAdapter, CodexAdapter, xterm.js, approvals, reconnection
- **Phase 3**: Multi-node, PWA push, SpacetimeDB discovery, self-hosting, Lago recording

## Linear Project

Tracked in Linear: [Life Relay — Remote Agent Sessions](https://linear.app/broomva/project/life-relay-remote-agent-sessions-e40523e79ad0)

Tickets: BRO-264 through BRO-280 (16 issues across 3 phases)

//! Relay error types.

use thiserror::Error;

#[derive(Debug, Error)]
pub enum RelayError {
    #[error("authentication failed: {0}")]
    Auth(String),

    #[error("connection failed: {0}")]
    Connection(String),

    #[error("session not found: {0}")]
    SessionNotFound(String),

    #[error("session spawn failed: {0}")]
    SpawnFailed(String),

    #[error("adapter error: {0}")]
    Adapter(String),

    #[error("protocol error: {0}")]
    Protocol(String),

    #[error("io error: {0}")]
    Io(#[from] std::io::Error),

    #[error("json error: {0}")]
    Json(#[from] serde_json::Error),
}

pub type RelayResult<T> = Result<T, RelayError>;

unsafe_code = "deny"

dbg_macro = "warn"

print_stderr = "warn"

unimplemented = "warn"

trivially_copy_pass_by_ref = "warn"

manual_let_else = "warn"
large_enum_variant = "warn"
doc_markdown = "warn"
semicolon_if_nothing_returned = "warn"
match_wildcard_for_single_variants = "warn"

[workspace.dependencies]
# Core
serde = { version = "1", features = ["derive"] }
serde_json = "1"
thiserror = "2"

uuid = { version = "1", features = ["v4", "serde"] }

tracing-subscriber = { version = "0.3", features = ["env-filter"] }

life-relay-api = { version = "0.1.0", path = "crates/life-relay-api" }

[profile.dev]
opt-level = 0
debug = true

opt-level = 3
lto = true
codegen-units = 1
strip = true
panic = "abort"

This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   - User (broomva via Discord) asked to continue Life Relay implementation from BRO-267, specifically: implement PTY integration, structured event parser, then session detail UI.
   - After completion, user said "Good, continue please" — meaning proceed to next tickets: BRO-292 (structured event parser detecting edits/bash/thinking from Claude TUI output) and BRO-293 (session detail page `/console/relay/session/[id]`).

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - **Life Relay**: Rust daemon (`relayd`) that bridges local agent sessions (Claude Code, Codex, Arcan) to broomva.tech web UI via HTTP polling
   - **portable-pty 0.9**: Required upgrade from 0.8 — v0.9 adds `Child: Send + Sync` and `MasterPty: Send`, enabling safe cross-thread PTY management
   - **SessionAdapter trait**: Async trait with `spawn(config, event_tx)`, `send_input`, `kill`, `resize` methods
   - **ClaudeAdapter**: Manages `HashMap<Uuid, PtyHandle>` in `Arc<RwLock>`, forwards PTY output as `DaemonMessage::Output` events and surfaces `ApprovalRequest` events
   - **PtyHandle**: Wraps portable-pty with background reader/writer tasks, `clone_killer()` for safe kill, keepalive master PTY arc
   - **ClaudeEvent parser**: Parses `claude --output-format stream-json` JSONL lines into typed variants
   - **SessionRegistry**: `Arc<RwLock<HashMap<Uuid, SessionInfo>>>` shared between daemon and local HTTP API
   - **HTTP polling relay**: `POST /api/relay/connect` (register), `GET /api/relay/poll` (get commands), `POST /api/relay/events` (push output)
   - **Linear project**: BRO-264 through BRO-280, with BRO-264/265/266/268/269/270 Done, BRO-267 just completed, BRO-271 In Progress, BRO-292/293 Backlog/High

3. Files and Code Sections:

   - **`relay/Cargo.toml`** — Workspace manifest; upgraded `portable-pty = "0.8"` → `"0.9"` to get `Child: Send + Sync` and `MasterPty: Send`

   - **`relay/crates/life-relayd/Cargo.toml`** — Added `portable-pty.workspace = true`

   - **`relay/crates/life-relayd/src/adapters/pty.rs`** (NEW):
     ```rust
     pub struct PtyHandle {
         pub id: Uuid,
         pub pid: Option<u32>,
         input_tx: mpsc::Sender<String>,
         killer: Arc<Mutex<Box<dyn portable_pty::ChildKiller + Send + Sync>>>,
         output_rx: Option<mpsc::Receiver<String>>,
         master: Arc<Mutex<Box<dyn portable_pty::MasterPty + Send>>>,
     }
     impl PtyHandle {
         pub fn spawn(id: Uuid, cmd: &[String], workdir: &str) -> Result<Self, RelayError>
         pub fn resize(&self, cols: u16, rows: u16) -> Result<(), RelayError>
         pub async fn send_input(&self, data: &str) -> Result<(), RelayError>
         pub fn kill(&self) -> Result<(), RelayError>
         pub fn take_output_rx(&mut self) -> mpsc::Receiver<String>
     }
     ```
     Key: drops child after getting PID + killer; master held in Arc to prevent SIGHUP; reader/writer in `spawn_blocking` tasks.

   - **`relay/crates/life-relayd/src/adapters/parser.rs`** (NEW):
     ```rust
     #![allow(dead_code)]
     pub enum ClaudeEvent {
         SystemInit { session_id: Option<String>, model: Option<String>, cwd: Option<String> },
         AssistantText { text: String },
         ToolUse { id: String, name: String, input: serde_json::Value },
         ToolResult { tool_use_id: Option<String>, content: String, is_error: bool },
         ApprovalRequest { approval_id: String, capability: String, context: String },
         Result { cost_usd: Option<f64>, duration_ms: Option<u64> },
         Raw(String),
     }
     pub fn parse_line(line: &str) -> ClaudeEvent
     ```
     Parses `--output-format stream-json` JSONL; falls back to `Raw` for ANSI/unknown.

   - **`relay/crates/life-relayd/src/adapters/mod.rs`** (UPDATED):
     ```rust
     #[async_trait::async_trait]
     pub trait SessionAdapter: Send + Sync {
         async fn spawn(&self, config: &SpawnConfig, event_tx: mpsc::Sender<DaemonMessage>) -> RelayResult<SessionInfo>;
         async fn send_input(&self, session_id: &Uuid, data: &str) -> RelayResult<()>;
         async fn kill(&self, session_id: &Uuid) -> RelayResult<()>;
         async fn resize(&self, session_id: &Uuid, cols: u16, rows: u16) -> RelayResult<()>;
     }
     pub mod claude; pub mod parser; pub mod pty;
     ```

   - **`relay/crates/life-relayd/src/adapters/claude.rs`** (REWRITTEN):
     - `ClaudeAdapter { sessions: Arc<RwLock<HashMap<Uuid, PtyHandle>>> }`
     - `build_command()` now uses `--output-format stream-json` (removed `--dangerously-skip-permissions` and Discord plugin flags)
     - `spawn()`: creates PtyHandle, starts background task forwarding raw output + parsed ApprovalRequest/Result events to `event_tx`
     - `Approve` routing: sends `y\n`/`n\n` to session stdin

   - **`relay/crates/life-relayd/src/daemon.rs`** (REWRITTEN):
     ```rust
     pub type SessionRegistry = Arc<RwLock<HashMap<uuid::Uuid, SessionInfo>>>;
     pub async fn run(bind: &str, server_url: &str) -> Result<()>
     async fn dispatch_command(msg: ServerMessage, adapter: &Arc<dyn SessionAdapter>, registry: &SessionRegistry, outbound: &mpsc::Sender<DaemonMessage>)
     ```
     - Real `ClaudeAdapter` replacing all mocks
     - Session registry shared between daemon and local API
     - Approve command routes `y\n`/`n\n` to session stdin

   - **`relay/crates/life-relay-api/src/lib.rs`** (UPDATED):
     ```rust
     pub type SessionRegistry = Arc<RwLock<HashMap<Uuid, SessionInfo>>>;
     #[derive(Clone)] pub struct AppState { pub sessions: SessionRegistry }
     pub fn build_router(state: AppState) -> Router
     // Routes: GET /health, GET /api/sessions, GET /api/sessions/{id}
     ```

   - **`relay/crates/life-relayd/src/main.rs`** (UPDATED): Replaced all `eprintln!` with `tracing` calls; fixed bare URL in doc comment

   - **`relay/crates/life-relayd/src/config.rs`**: Added `#[allow(dead_code)]` to `registry_path()`

   - **`relay/crates/life-relayd/src/connection.rs`**: Fixed doc backtick on `node_id`, collapsed nested `if` into `&&`

   - **`~/.claude/projects/-Users-broomva-broomva-core-life/memory/project_relay_handoff.md`** (NEW): Handoff memory documenting completed work and next steps

4. Errors and fixes:
   - **portable-pty 0.8 `Child` not `Send`**: `Box<dyn Child>` in 0.8 is not `Send` because the trait doesn't have `+ Send`. Fixed by upgrading to 0.9 where `Child: Send + Sync` and `MasterPty: Send`.
   - **17 clippy errors with `-D warnings`**: Fixed:
     - `unused_mut` on child variable → removed `mut`
     - `list_sessions` dead code → removed from trait
     - `ClaudeEvent` fields dead code → added `#![allow(dead_code)]` to parser module
     - `registry_path` dead code → `#[allow(dead_code)]`
     - Redundant closures (`|v| v.as_bool()` etc.) → replaced with `serde_json::Value::as_bool`
     - Bare URL in doc → wrapped in `<>`
     - Nested `if` → collapsed with `&&`
     - `eprintln!` (print_stderr lint) → replaced with `tracing` calls
   - **`build_router()` signature mismatch**: API previously took no args; daemon called it with state → updated both

5. Problem Solving:
   - **Core challenge**: `Box<dyn portable_pty::Child>` not `Send` in v0.8 — solved by upgrading to v0.9
   - **Master PTY keepalive**: Dropping the master PTY sends SIGHUP to child; solved by holding it in `Arc<Mutex<Box<dyn MasterPty + Send>>>` within `PtyHandle`
   - **Child lifecycle**: Dropped `Box<dyn Child>` after getting PID + `clone_killer()` handle; acceptable zombie for MVP since relayd exit reaps them
   - **Approval routing**: `Approve` server message routes `y\n`/`n\n` to session stdin (Claude Code reads from terminal)

6. All user messages:
   - Discord: "Continue Life Relay implementation from BRO-267. Check memory/project_relay_handoff.md for exact state. Implement PTY integration, structured event parser, then session detail UI"
   - Discord: "Good, continue please"

7. Pending Tasks:
   - **BRO-292** (High): Structured event parser in ClaudeAdapter — detect Claude Code TUI output patterns: `⏺ Edit(path)`, `⏺ Bash(cmd)`, `· Thinking...`, markdown responses → new `DaemonMessage` variants (`FileEdit`, `BashCommand`, `Thinking`, `MarkdownResponse`)
   - **BRO-293** (High): Session detail page `/console/relay/session/[id]` — SSE event stream, structured event cards (file-edit, bash-command, thinking, assistant-message), input bar, approval dialog
   - **BRO-271** (In Progress): Console pages — basic `/console/relay` done, needs session detail view
   - **BRO-267**: Mark as Done in Linear (was just completed)

1→//! Wire protocol types for WebSocket communication between relayd and server.
     2→//!
     3→//! All messages serialize as JSON. The WebSocket carries these bidirectionally:
     4→//! - `ServerMessage`: commands from the web UI → daemon
     5→//! - `DaemonMessage`: events from local sessions → server → browser
     6→
     7→use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
     8→use uuid::Uuid;
     9→
    10→use crate::session::{SessionInfo, SessionType, SpawnConfig};
    11→
    12→// ── Server → Daemon ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    13→
    14→/// Commands sent from broomva.tech to the relay daemon.
    15→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
    16→#[serde(tag = "type", rename_all = "snake_case")]
    17→pub enum ServerMessage {
    18→    /// Spawn a new agent session.
    19→    Spawn {
    20→        session_type: SessionType,
    21→        config: SpawnConfig,
    22→    },
    23→    /// Send input (text or keystrokes) to a session.
    24→    Input { session_id: Uuid, data: String },
    25→    /// Resize the PTY for a session.
    26→    Resize {
    27→        session_id: Uuid,
    28→        cols: u16,
    29→        rows: u16,
    30→    },
    31→    /// Resolve a capability approval request.
    32→    Approve {
    33→        session_id: Uuid,
    34→        approval_id: String,
    35→        approved: bool,
    36→    },
    37→    /// Kill a session.
    38→    Kill { session_id: Uuid },
    39→    /// Request the current session list.
    40→    ListSessions,
    41→    /// Keepalive ping.
    42→    Ping,
    43→}
    44→
    45→// ── Daemon → Server ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    46→
    47→/// Events sent from the relay daemon to broomva.tech.
    48→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
    49→#[serde(tag = "type", rename_all = "snake_case")]
    50→pub enum DaemonMessage {
    51→    /// Output data from a session (terminal bytes or structured events).
    52→    Output {
    53→        session_id: Uuid,
    54→        data: String,
    55→        seq: u64,
    56→    },
    57→    /// A new session was created.
    58→    SessionCreated { session: SessionInfo },
    59→    /// A session has ended.
    60→    SessionEnded { session_id: Uuid, reason: String },
    61→    /// A session needs capability approval from the user.
    62→    ApprovalRequest {
    63→        session_id: Uuid,
    64→        approval_id: String,
    65→        capability: String,
    66→        context: String,
    67→    },
    68→    /// Response to `ListSessions`.
    69→    SessionList { sessions: Vec<SessionInfo> },
    70→    /// Node identification sent on connect.
    71→    NodeInfo {
    72→        name: String,
    73→        hostname: String,
    74→        capabilities: Vec<String>,
    75→    },
    76→    /// Keepalive pong.
    77→    Pong,
    78→    /// Error message.
    79→    Error { code: String, message: String },
    80→}
    81→
    82→#[cfg(test)]
    83→mod tests {
    84→    use super::*;
    85→
    86→    #[test]
    87→    fn server_message_roundtrip() {
    88→        let msg = ServerMessage::Input {
    89→            session_id: Uuid::new_v4(),
    90→            data: "hello".to_string(),
    91→        };
    92→        let json = serde_json::to_string(&msg).unwrap();
    93→        let parsed: ServerMessage = serde_json::from_str(&json).unwrap();
    94→        assert!(matches!(parsed, ServerMessage::Input { .. }));
    95→    }
    96→
    97→    #[test]
    98→    fn daemon_message_roundtrip() {
    99→        let msg = DaemonMessage::Output {
   100→            session_id: Uuid::new_v4(),
   101→            data: "test output".to_string(),
   102→            seq: 42,
   103→        };
   104→        let json = serde_json::to_string(&msg).unwrap();
   105→        let parsed: DaemonMessage = serde_json::from_str(&json).unwrap();
   106→        assert!(matches!(parsed, DaemonMessage::Output { seq: 42, .. }));
   107→    }
   108→
   109→    #[test]
   110→    fn node_info_serialization() {
   111→        let msg = DaemonMessage::NodeInfo {
   112→            name: "macbook-pro".to_string(),
   113→            hostname: "MacBook-Pro.local".to_string(),
   114→            capabilities: vec!["claude-code".to_string(), "arcan".to_string()],
   115→        };
   116→        let json = serde_json::to_string_pretty(&msg).unwrap();
   117→        assert!(json.contains("node_info"));
   118→        assert!(json.contains("claude-code"));
   119→    }
   120→}

1→//! Session types shared between daemon and server.
     2→
     3→use chrono::{DateTime, Utc};
     4→use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
     5→use uuid::Uuid;
     6→
     7→/// Type of agent session.
     8→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
     9→#[serde(rename_all = "kebab-case")]
    10→pub enum SessionType {
    11→    Arcan,
    12→    ClaudeCode,
    13→    Codex,
    14→}
    15→
    16→impl std::fmt::Display for SessionType {
    17→    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {
    18→        match self {
    19→            Self::Arcan => write!(f, "arcan"),
    20→            Self::ClaudeCode => write!(f, "claude-code"),
    21→            Self::Codex => write!(f, "codex"),
    22→        }
    23→    }
    24→}
    25→
    26→/// Session lifecycle status.
    27→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
    28→#[serde(rename_all = "lowercase")]
    29→pub enum SessionStatus {
    30→    Active,
    31→    Idle,
    32→    Completed,
    33→    Failed,
    34→}
    35→
    36→/// Information about a session on the relay node.
    37→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
    38→#[serde(rename_all = "camelCase")]
    39→pub struct SessionInfo {
    40→    pub id: Uuid,
    41→    pub session_type: SessionType,
    42→    pub status: SessionStatus,
    43→    pub name: String,
    44→    pub workdir: String,
    45→    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    46→    pub model: Option<String>,
    47→    pub created_at: DateTime<Utc>,
    48→}
    49→
    50→/// Configuration for spawning a new session.
    51→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
    52→#[serde(rename_all = "camelCase")]
    53→pub struct SpawnConfig {
    54→    pub name: String,
    55→    #[serde(default = "default_workdir")]
    56→    pub workdir: String,
    57→    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    58→    pub model: Option<String>,
    59→    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    60→    pub session_id: Option<Uuid>,
    61→}
    62→
    63→fn default_workdir() -> String {
    64→    std::env::var("HOME").unwrap_or_else(|_| ".".to_string())
    65→}

input.text:
Relay backlog, prioritized by impact:

**High (MVP completion)**
- **BRO-275** — xterm.js terminal rendering. Right now the session view renders raw PTY bytes as `<pre>` text — no ANSI colors, no cursor positioning, no resize. Replacing it with xterm.js makes it an actual working terminal. This is the biggest gap between "functional" and "usable".
- **BRO-276** — Session persistence + reconnect. Buffer last N output events in Redis by seq number, replay on browser reconnect. Right now refreshing the page loses all session history.

**Medium (polish)**
- **BRO-274** — Approval flow is already wired (BRO-293 did it), but the ticket wants the full end-to-end: Arcan's `ApprovalPort` integration + keystroke routing. The web UI half is done.
- **BRO-294** — Workspace sidebar: session picker, model selector, spawn new session button, git status panel.

**Lower priority**
- **BRO-277** — Multi-node support + node health monitoring (mark offline after 60s heartbeat gap)
- **BRO-278** — PWA push notifications for approval requests
- **BRO-280** — Self-hosting docs + Docker compose
- **BRO-279** — SpacetimeDB integration for node discovery

Also need to mark BRO-292, BRO-293, and BRO-271 as Done in Linear.

**My recommendation: BRO-275 next.** xterm.js turns the session page from a proof-of-concept into something you'd actually want to use.
